race.name: Ski to the Sun
race.tagline: Forty-two kilometers through the Methow Valley's 200-kilometer trail network — North America's Nordic paradise, finishing at Sun Mountain Lodge.

vitals.aid_stations: 3-4 stations with water, sports drink, snacks

climate.description: The Methow Valley sits in the rain shadow of the Cascades, receiving reliable dry snowfall. Race day temperatures typically range from -10°C to 0°C. Blue skies are common, but Arctic outflow events can bring extreme cold.
challenges: Possible Arctic outflow creating bitter cold and wind
Afternoon sun softening trails at lower elevations
Variable wax conditions across elevation changes
Remote valley location complicates logistics if weather turns

course.primary: Point-to-point through the Methow Valley trail system, traversing forest, meadow, and river valley terrain. Finishes with a climb up to Sun Mountain Lodge at 3,000 feet with panoramic views of the North Cascades.
course.surface: Machine-groomed trails on North America's largest cross-country ski trail network
course.grooming: Excellent grooming standard, both classic and skate lanes maintained by Methow Trails
features: Point-to-point finish at Sun Mountain Lodge with panoramic views
North America's largest groomed trail network (200+ km)
Final climb to Sun Mountain Lodge is a signature challenge

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: World-class grooming on North America's largest trail network. Young race with small but passionate field. Remote location limits accessibility but rewards with stunning North Cascades scenery.

history.summary: Founded in 2019, Ski to the Sun is a relatively new race that leverages the Methow Valley's extraordinary trail infrastructure. The Methow Trails system, maintained by a dedicated nonprofit, is the largest groomed cross-country ski trail network in North America with over 200 kilometers of trails. The race quickly gained a cult following, selling out annually as word spread about the spectacular point-to-point course finishing at the iconic Sun Mountain Lodge.
notable_facts: The Methow Valley has over 200 km of groomed trails — the largest network in North America
Sells out every year despite the race's youth
Sun Mountain Lodge finish provides one of the most scenic race endings in Nordic skiing
The Methow Trails nonprofit maintains the entire network through community support
The valley sits in the Cascade rain shadow, creating reliable cold, dry snow

youtube_data.search_query: Ski to the Sun cross country ski race Methow Valley

videos.title: Ski To the Sun 2022 - Final Version
videos.description:
Hosted by the Methow Trails and Methow Valley Nordic Skiing Educational Foundation, this marathon length race stretches the length of the Methow Valley from Mazama up to Sun Mountain.  41+ km with a rigorous uphill the last 10 kilometers.

Filmed and edited in under 12 hours.  
Telegraphix Studios in Winthrop, Washington

videos.title: Ski to the Sun 2025
videos.description: An aerial collage of the race route of the Ski to the Sun race in the Methow Valley, hosted by the Methow Trails.  Most of the footage is from the race on 8 Feb 2025.  The race results are posted here: https://zone4.ca/event/2025/VEBoNR//results/VR29kV/

Popular destinations for overnight hut camping the Rendezvous trails cover valley floor to high-forested ridges above Methow Valley and the Cub Creek side.

videos.title: Ski to the Sun Ski Marathon & Relay 2019
videos.description: A 42km XC ski race in the Methow Valley, WA. Individual and relay options. Part of the American Ski Marathon Series. For more information visit https://www.methowtrails.org/events/2019-ski-to-the-sun-marathon-and-relay/01-26-2019
tags: Cross Country Skiing american ski marathon series Methow Valley MVNSEF Cross country ski racing

videos.title: 2013 Gatineau Loppet 55k Classic Race Review
videos.description:
ActiveSteve, not even recovered from tackling the Canadian Ski Marathon the week before, heads back out with his skis on to cover another classic Canadian ski event, the Gatineau Loppet, heading into it's 35th year. To commemorate the 35th year, the Classic event featured a 55km and 38km linear (point-to-point) route instead of the normal looped course. Under brilliant skies and great snow conditions, he lined up with participants from around the world. Check out the video to see how is all unrolled.

Music: Dan-O Songs

quotes.text: I'm worried about the waxes it might get hot in the afternoon... this race is a bit unique in that it ends with a giant climb and I knew that was coming and was prepped for that but it was also a really fun way if you if you still have some legs left at the end you can kind of crank the gear one more and push it all the way to the end and some good Technical downhills and Corners too which is is pretty unique in a big Marathon course like this.

We had the great conditions out there the first half was super fast was fun skiing in a pack then when we got to the climb things got a bit more challenging snow slowed down a little bit but man epic scenery great people cheering out there love the love the spirit of the race.
It was so fun and beautiful to ski through the trees and to end that's such a perfect place like the Sun Mountain Lodge... we could not have hoped for more perfect weather or more perfect tracks.
